# cloudinaryprovisioning_custom_policy (Resource)

Manages a Cloudinary custom permissions policy. Custom policies allow fine-grained access control over Cloudinary resources using Cedar policy language.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "cloudinaryprovisioning_product_environment" "example" {
  cloud_name = "my-project-cloud"
}

resource "cloudinaryprovisioning_access_key" "example" {
  product_environment_id = data.cloudinaryprovisioning_product_environment.example.id
  name                   = "my-system-api-key"
}

resource "cloudinaryprovisioning_custom_policy" "example" {
  name       = "my-project-policy"
  scope_type = "prodenv"
  scope_id   = data.cloudinaryprovisioning_product_environment.example.id
  enabled    = true

  policy_statement = <<-EOT
    permit(
      principal == Cloudinary::APIKey::"${cloudinaryprovisioning_access_key.example.api_key}",
      action in [Cloudinary::Action::"create", Cloudinary::Action::"update", Cloudinary::Action::"read", Cloudinary::Action::"delete"],
      resource is Cloudinary::Asset
    );
  EOT
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The display name of the custom policy.
- `policy_statement` (String) The Cedar policy statement string.
- `scope_type` (String) The scope of the policy. Must be one of: "account" (applies to the whole account) or "prodenv" (applies to a specific product environment).

### Optional

- `description` (String) A human-readable description of the custom policy.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the custom policy is enabled.
- `scope_id` (String) The ID of the product environment this policy applies to. Required when scope_type is "prodenv". Must be omitted or empty when scope_type is "account".

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) The Unix timestamp at which the custom policy was created.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the custom policy assigned by Cloudinary.
- `updated_at` (String) The Unix timestamp at which the custom policy was last updated.
